ideas.title: Your First CQ on CW: What Nobody Warns You About
ideas.why_now: A viral Hacker News thread (77 comments) and a relatable Mastodon post about nearly switching off the radio mid-QSO show CW anxiety is a live conversation.
ideas.angle: Walk through the emotional reality of calling CQ for the first time on Morse code, from the panic when someone answers to the muscle memory that kicks in. Practical tips for new CW ops, not theory.

ideas.title: ITU Corporation Is Buying Up Ham Radio Brands. Should You Care?
ideas.why_now: ITU Corp acquired Ameritron, Mirage, Cushcraft, and Hygain in rapid succession, a consolidation story with real implications for gear buyers.
ideas.angle: Break down what this brand roll-up means for everyday hams buying amplifiers and antennas. Are parts and support going to get better or worse? What should you buy (or stock up on) now?

sources.label: QRZ Forums, Ameritron and Mirage acquisition
QRZ Forums, Cushcraft and Hygain acquisition

ideas.title: POTA Support Your Parks Weekend: How to Join In
ideas.why_now: POTA activations are trending (velocity 1.5) and Support Your Parks Weekend just ran, with multiple activation reports and club outings posted this week.
ideas.angle: Give listeners a practical on-ramp for Support Your Parks Weekend, covering how the event differs from a regular activation, why hunting counts too, and how to drag your club along for the fun.

sources.label: Mastodon, POTA Support Your Parks Weekend

ideas.title: Fox Hunting Is Back: How to Run an ARDF Event at Your Club
ideas.why_now: EMF Camp 2026 ran a well-attended amateur radio direction finding event with borrowed receiver kits, sparking fresh interest in accessible fox hunting setups.
ideas.angle: Show clubs how to put on a fox hunt without a big budget, using the EMF Camp model as inspiration. Cover basic equipment, hiding transmitters, and why new hams love it.

ideas.title: Getting on the Air When You Don't Know What to Say
ideas.why_now: A new practical guide on on-air etiquette and conversation scripts was published this week, filling a gap that beginners ask about constantly.
ideas.angle: Use real scripts and net formats to help shy or new operators get past the fear of keying up. Cover simplex calls, joining a net, and what to do when the repeater goes quiet.

ideas.title: QO-100: The Geostationary Satellite Anyone Can Use for HF
ideas.why_now: A multi-year QO-100 build just reached completion and was shared this week, and satellite operating demos at EMF Camp drew real crowds, signaling growing curiosity.
ideas.angle: Demystify QO-100 for operators who think satellites are out of reach. Cover what gear you actually need, what a basic station looks like, and what operating through a geosynchronous bird feels like.

sources.label: Mastodon, BATC satellite demos at EMF Camp

ideas.title: High-Altitude Balloon Launches: Ham Radio's Best Science Project
ideas.why_now: The K5NSX balloon launch recap posted this week is a great concrete example of a club HAB project that mixes radio tracking, telemetry, and community.
ideas.angle: Walk through what a HAB launch actually involves from a ham radio perspective, covering APRS tracking, recovery, and how a small club can pull one off without a massive budget.

ideas.title: The Lee De Forest Special Event: Why This One Is Worth Chasing
ideas.why_now: K6L runs August 7 to 9, 2026, honoring the inventor of the triode vacuum tube. A timely hook for a story about radio's roots that everyday hams can actually get on the air for.
ideas.angle: Preview the K6L special event, explain who De Forest was and why his work matters to every ham alive, and give practical tips for working the station and collecting the award.

sources.label: QRZ Forums, K6L Lee De Forest Special Event
